name: BRICS+
climate: Highly diverse: tropical, arid, temperate, continental, arctic, monsoon — spans all major climate zones

neighbors.name: Shanghai Cooperation Organisation
neighbors.relevance: Heavy membership overlap with Russia, China, India, Iran; shared security and economic agenda

neighbors.name: Group of Twenty
neighbors.relevance: All BRICS members are G20 members; competing visions for global economic governance

neighbors.name: OPEC+
neighbors.relevance: Saudi Arabia, UAE, Russia, Iran are both BRICS+ and OPEC+; energy policy coordination

neighbors.name: African Union
neighbors.relevance: Ethiopia, Egypt, South Africa bridge BRICS to the African continent

neighbors.name: ASEAN
neighbors.relevance: China and India are major ASEAN trade partners; BRICS expansion targets Southeast Asia

neighbors.name: Gulf Cooperation Council
neighbors.relevance: Saudi Arabia and UAE membership links BRICS to Gulf petrodollar economies

links.description: BRICS positions itself as counterweight to G7-led global order; dedollarization agenda
Shared members (China, Russia, India, Iran) and parallel summit diplomacy
BRICS energy producers coordinate oil output; influence global energy pricing
China's BRI infrastructure spans BRICS member states; development finance linkage
Iran, Saudi Arabia, UAE energy exports transit Hormuz; critical for BRICS oil trade
China bridges BRICS and RCEP; overlapping trade liberalization frameworks
Egypt membership gives BRICS direct stake in Suez transit chokepoint
South Africa controls alternative route for BRICS commodity shipments
